proofs.proof_tex: Fix n, a unit i, and a target label h. For a monomial a in M_ihn, let C_a be the event that every signed literal in a is satisfied by W_n. By def:literal-map, def:incidence-polynomial, and def:exposure-event, E_ihn=1 exactly when at least one C_a with a in M_ihn occurs, because P_ihn(W_n) is the sum of the literal products and each product is a 0-1 indicator. Hence pi_ihn=Pr_pi(union_{a in M_ihn} C_a). Since M_ihn is finite, the ordinary inclusion-exclusion identity gives pi_ihn=sum_{emptyset != A subseteq M_ihn} (-1)^(|A|+1) Pr_pi(intersection_{a in A} C_a). The event intersection_{a in A} C_a is the event that every literal appearing in the union of the monomials in A is satisfied. By lem:literal-conjunction-probability, its probability is chi(A) p_n^(u(A)) (1-p_n)^(v(A)). Substitution gives the displayed first-exposure formula. For the joint formula, fix i != j and labels h,h'. Write C_a for monomials a in M_ihn and D_b for monomials b in M_jh'n. The event {E_ihn=1,E_jh'n=1} is (union_a C_a) intersect (union_b D_b). Expanding both union indicators by inclusion-exclusion and multiplying gives the coefficient (-1)^(|A|+1)(-1)^(|B|+1)=(-1)^(|A|+|B|) for each nonempty A subseteq M_ihn and nonempty B subseteq M_jh'n. The intersection event requires all literals in A union B. Applying lem:literal-conjunction-probability to A union B gives chi(A union B) p_n^(u(A union B)) (1-p_n)^(v(A union B)). Summing over A and B proves the joint formula.

Let X_in=psi_in-E_pi[psi_in]. By ass:exposure-consistency, on the event E_ihn=1 the observed outcome equals Y_in(h), so the two target terms in psi_in can be written as E_ih_n^+n Y_in(h_n^+)/pi_ih_n^+n and E_ih_n^-n Y_in(h_n^-)/pi_ih_n^-n. The potential outcomes and probabilities are fixed finite-population constants. By def:incidence-polynomial and def:exposure-event, E_ihn is a measurable function only of the cluster assignments W_cn with c in B_ihn. Therefore X_in is measurable with respect to the sigma-field generated by {W_cn: c in B_in}. If two disjoint vertex sets A and C in U_n have no edge between them in G_n^dep, then B_in intersect B_jn is empty for every i in A and j in C, so the two unions of cluster supports are disjoint. Under ass:bernoulli-ego-design the assignment variables on disjoint cluster sets are independent, hence {X_in: i in A} and {X_jn: j in C} are independent. This is exactly the dependency-graph property for G_n^dep. For the degree bound, fix i. Every neighbor j of i shares at least one cluster c in B_in. For a fixed c, at most omega_n target-contrast units have c in their contrast support, so at most omega_n-1 of them are neighbors of i through c. A union bound over c in B_in gives deg(i) <= |B_in|(omega_n-1) <= b_n(omega_n-1). Taking the maximum over i gives Delta_n <= b_n(omega_n-1). Finally, for each target label h, B_ihn is the union of the cluster indices appearing in at most kappa_n monomials, each of cardinality at most q_n, so |B_ihn| <= q_n kappa_n. Since B_in = B_ih_n^+n union B_ih_n^-n, |B_in| <= 2 q_n kappa_n, hence b_n <= 2 q_n kappa_n and Delta_n <= b_n(omega_n-1) <= 2 q_n kappa_n(omega_n-1).

Let X_in=n^(-1)(psi_in-E_pi[psi_in]) and S_n=sum_{i=1}^n X_in. By lem:ht-unbiased-bounded and def:ht-contrast, S_n=hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n and Var_pi(S_n)=sigma_n^2. The same lemma gives |psi_in-E_pi[psi_in]| <= C_Y with C_Y=4 M_Y/pi_min, hence |X_in| <= C_Y/n, sum_i E_pi[X_in^4] <= C_Y^4 n^(-3), and sum_i E_pi[|X_in|^3] <= C_Y^3 n^(-2). By prop:dependency-degree, the X_in have dependency graph G_n^dep with maximum degree Delta_n. Apply the cited dependency-graph normal approximation lem:dependency-graph-normal-bound with d=Delta_n, S=S_n, and sigma^2=sigma_n^2. Since ass:variance-nondegenerate gives sigma_n^2 >= c_sigma/n, the first Stein term is at most K_1 Delta_n^(3/2) n^(-1/2), and the second is at most K_2 Delta_n^2 n^(-1/2), where K_1,K_2 depend only on M_Y, pi_min, c_sigma, and the universal constants in lem:dependency-graph-normal-bound. By ass:primitive-degree-rate, Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)); therefore Delta_n^(3/2)n^(-1/2)=o(n^(-1/8)) and Delta_n^2 n^(-1/2)=o(1). Thus d_W((hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n)/sigma_n,Z) -> 0 for Z standard normal. A standard Lipschitz smoothing of half-lines yields convergence in distribution to N(0,1): for any continuity point t of the normal cdf and any epsilon>0, approximate 1{x<=t} from above and below by 1/epsilon-Lipschitz functions that differ only on [t-epsilon,t+epsilon], use the Wasserstein bound, and then let epsilon down to zero. This proves (hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n)/sigma_n => N(0,1). For coverage, set z=z_(1-alpha/2). By def:oracle-ci, the event {tau_n in CI_n^orc(1-alpha)} is {|(hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n)/sigma_n| <= z}. Since z is a continuity point of the standard normal law, the just-proved convergence gives Pr_pi{|(hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n)/sigma_n| <= z} -> Phi(z)-Phi(-z)=1-alpha. This proves oracle Wald coverage.

Let y^0_{i+}=Y_in(h_n^+) and y^0_{i-}=Y_in(h_n^-). By ass:bounded-outcomes, y^0 lies in [-M_Y,M_Y]^(2n). By ass:exposure-consistency, whenever E_{ir}=1 for r in {+,-}, the observed outcome equals the corresponding target potential outcome, so y^0 satisfies all equality constraints defining F_n^obs. Hence y^0 in F_n^obs for every realized assignment and observed target-exposure dataset generated by the array. By lem:variance-quadratic-exact, Q_n(y^0)=sigma_n^2. Since Vbar_n is the maximum of Q_n over F_n^obs in def:variance-envelope-handle, Vbar_n >= Q_n(y^0)=sigma_n^2 pathwise.

Conditional on the realized exposure indicators and outcomes, F_n^obs is the subset of R^(2n) defined by the coordinate bounds -M_Y <= y_{ir} <= M_Y and the linear equalities y_{ir}=Y_in^obs for coordinates with E_{ir}=1. Thus it is a closed bounded finite-dimensional box with equality constraints; in particular it is compact, and it is nonempty for generated datasets because the true bounded target-potential vector is feasible by ass:exposure-consistency and ass:bounded-outcomes. The objective Q_n(y) in def:variance-envelope-handle is a finite quadratic polynomial in y whose coefficients are the design probabilities pi_{ir}, pi_{ij}^{rs} and signs s_r. The probabilities entering those coefficients are computable from the signed incidence families and p_n by prop:incidence-probabilities, with the diagonal convention stated in def:variance-envelope-handle. Hence Vbar_n is an explicit observed-data quadratic optimization problem with 2n variables, box constraints, and equality constraints. Continuity of Q_n on the compact feasible set gives attainment by Weierstrass' theorem, and the displayed finite-dimensional optimization is the claimed computable observed-data functional.

Fix a realized observed target-exposure dataset and consider the bounded completion class F_n^obs from def:variance-envelope-handle. For each completion y in F_n^obs, lem:variance-quadratic-exact identifies Q_n(y) as the design variance that the HT contrast would have if y were the vector of target potential outcomes. By definition Vbar_n=max_{y in F_n^obs} Q_n(y). Therefore Vbar_n itself dominates the variance for every bounded completion. Conversely, let U be any observed-data upper envelope that dominates sigma_n^2 for every bounded target-potential completion consistent with the same observed data. Applying this domination property to each y in F_n^obs gives U >= Q_n(y) for all y in F_n^obs, hence U >= sup_{y in F_n^obs} Q_n(y)=Vbar_n. Thus no smaller observed-data upper envelope can dominate all completions, and Vbar_n is pointwise smallest.

For an array A in the subclass C, set X_i(A)=n^(-1)(psi_i(A)-E_{pi,A} psi_i(A)) and S_A=sum_i X_i(A). The proof of thm:ego-overlap-clt, using lem:ht-unbiased-bounded, prop:dependency-degree, ass:variance-nondegenerate, and lem:dependency-graph-normal-bound, gives constants K_1,K_2 independent of A such that d_W(S_A/sigma_n(A),Z) <= K_1 Delta_n(A)^(3/2)n^(-1/2)+K_2 Delta_n(A)^2 n^(-1/2). Let rho_n^Delta(C)=sup_{A in C} Delta_n(A)/n^(1/4). Taking suprema over A in C yields sup_A d_W(S_A/sigma_n(A),Z) <= K_1 (rho_n^Delta(C))^(3/2)n^(-1/8)+K_2 (rho_n^Delta(C))^2, which tends to zero by ass:uniform-primitive-degree-rate. Let z=z_(1-alpha/2). For epsilon in (0,z), choose a Lipschitz function f_epsilon with 0 <= f_epsilon <= 1{|x|<=z}, f_epsilon(x)=1 for |x|<=z-epsilon, and Lipschitz constant at most 1/epsilon. Then uniformly in A, Pr_{pi,A}{|S_A| <= z sigma_n(A)} >= E f_epsilon(S_A/sigma_n(A)) >= E f_epsilon(Z)-epsilon^(-1) sup_A d_W(S_A/sigma_n(A),Z) >= Pr{|Z|<=z-epsilon}-epsilon^(-1) sup_A d_W(S_A/sigma_n(A),Z). Taking infimum over A, then liminf_n, gives at least Pr{|Z|<=z-epsilon}. Finally let epsilon down to zero and use continuity of the normal cdf to obtain liminf_n inf_{A in C} Pr_{pi,A}{|hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n| <= z sigma_n(A)} >= Pr{|Z|<=z}=1-alpha. The identity S_A=hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n follows from def:ht-contrast and lem:ht-unbiased-bounded.

First fix a single sequence in def:clt-qualified-ego-incidence-array-class and write z=z_(1-alpha/2). By lem:variance-envelope-computable-attained, Vbar_n is an observed-data computable maximum. By lem:variance-envelope-dominates, Vbar_n >= sigma_n^2 pathwise for every realized assignment, so sqrt(Vbar_n) >= sigma_n. Therefore the oracle event {|hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n| <= z sigma_n} is pathwise contained in the feasible event {|hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n| <= z sqrt(Vbar_n)}. Taking probabilities and then liminf gives liminf_n Pr_pi{|hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n| <= z sqrt(Vbar_n)} >= liminf_n Pr_pi{|hat_tau_HT_n-tau_n| <= z sigma_n}. The right side equals 1-alpha by thm:ego-overlap-clt. This proves pointwise conservative coverage. For a subclass C satisfying ass:uniform-primitive-degree-rate, the same pathwise containment holds for every array A in C, hence inf_A Pr_A(feasible event) >= inf_A Pr_A(oracle event). Taking liminf and applying lem:uniform-oracle-wald-coverage gives the uniform lower bound 1-alpha. Finally, lem:variance-envelope-sharp states that Vbar_n is the pointwise smallest observed-data upper envelope for sigma_n^2 over bounded target-potential completions consistent with the realized target-exposure data. Since the Wald half-length z sqrt(V) is increasing in any nonnegative variance envelope V, Vbar_n gives the pointwise shortest conservative Wald interval within the observed-data variance-envelope Wald family for this fixed HT contrast. This is the asserted sharp conservative interpretation, and it is distinct from any claim of global minimality over all variance procedures.

Under the stated singleton condition, for each i and target label h the family M_ihn has exactly one monomial a_ih, and a_ih consists of exactly one signed literal (c_i(h),sigma_i(h)). The incidence polynomial is therefore P_ihn(W_n)=L_{c_i(h),sigma_i(h),n}(W_n), so E_ihn=1 exactly when that single literal is satisfied. Thus each target exposure event is a single signed literal. Its support B_ihn is the singleton {c_i(h)}, and the contrast support B_in is the union of the treated-label and reference-label singleton supports, so |B_in| <= 2 and b_n <= 2. In the inclusion-exclusion formula of prop:incidence-probabilities, the only nonempty subfamily is {a_ih}; it is consistent, and u_i(h) is one when sigma_i(h)=+1 and zero otherwise, while v_i(h) is one when sigma_i(h)=-1 and zero otherwise. Therefore pi_ihn=p_n^(u_i(h))(1-p_n)^(v_i(h)). The degree bound in prop:dependency-degree now gives Delta_n <= b_n(omega_n-1) <= 2(omega_n-1). Substituting these simplifications into thm:ego-overlap-clt leaves the usual Horvitz-Thompson contrast with bounded summands that depend only on a bounded number of assignment literals and whose dependence is encoded by the overlap dependency graph. In particular, when the overlap degree is bounded, the growing-degree condition Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)) is automatic, so the result is exactly the standard dependency-graph HT CLT for a bounded-degree exposure map.

added_lemmas.statement: Under ass:bernoulli-ego-design, for any finite nonempty collection C of signed monomials, the probability that every literal appearing in the union of C is satisfied equals chi(C) p_n^(u(C)) (1-p_n)^(v(C)).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: If the union of literals in C contains both (c,+1) and (c,-1) for some cluster c, then the event requires W_cn=1 and W_cn=0 simultaneously, so its probability is zero. In this case chi(C)=0, and the displayed expression is zero. If the union is consistent, it requires W_cn=1 for exactly u(C) distinct clusters and W_cn=0 for exactly v(C) distinct clusters, with no other restrictions. By ass:bernoulli-ego-design the W_cn are mutually independent Bernoulli(p_n), so the probability is p_n^(u(C))(1-p_n)^(v(C)). Since chi(C)=1 in the consistent case, this is chi(C) p_n^(u(C))(1-p_n)^(v(C)).

added_lemmas.statement: Under ass:exposure-consistency, ass:bounded-outcomes, and ass:positivity, E_pi[psi_in] = Y_in(h_n^+) - Y_in(h_n^-) for every i, hat_tau_HT_n is unbiased for tau_n, and |psi_in - E_pi[psi_in]| <= 4 M_Y / pi_min.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: On the event E_ihn=1, ass:exposure-consistency gives Y_in^obs=Y_in(h), because at most one exposure label is realized. Therefore E_pi[1{E_ihn=1}Y_in^obs/pi_ihn]=Y_in(h) Pr_pi(E_ihn=1)/pi_ihn=Y_in(h). Applying this identity to h_n^+ and h_n^- gives E_pi[psi_in]=Y_in(h_n^+)-Y_in(h_n^-). Averaging over i and using def:ht-contrast gives E_pi[hat_tau_HT_n]=tau_n. For boundedness, ass:bounded-outcomes and ass:positivity imply |psi_in| <= 2 M_Y/pi_min, while |E_pi psi_in| <= |Y_in(h_n^+)|+|Y_in(h_n^-)| <= 2 M_Y. Since pi_min <= 1, |psi_in-E_pi psi_in| <= 2 M_Y/pi_min+2 M_Y <= 4 M_Y/pi_min.

added_lemmas.statement: Let X_1,...,X_N be centered random variables with dependency graph of maximum degree d, let S=sum_i X_i, and let sigma^2=Var(S)>0. There are universal constants C_1,C_2 such that, for Z standard normal, d_W(S/sigma,Z) <= C_1 d^(3/2) sigma^(-2) (sum_i E[X_i^4])^(1/2) + C_2 d^2 sigma^(-3) sum_i E[|X_i|^3].

source.locator: Lemma 1, dependency-graph Stein bound; also recorded in the project graph as the Ross 2011 Theorem 3.6 / Chin 2018 Lemma 1 substrate

added_lemmas.proof_tex: Let X_1,...,X_N be centered random variables with dependency graph of maximum degree d, let S=sum_i X_i, and let sigma^2=Var(S)>0. There are universal constants C_1,C_2 such that, for Z standard normal, d_W(S/sigma,Z) <= C_1 d^(3/2) sigma^(-2) (sum_i E[X_i^4])^(1/2) + C_2 d^2 sigma^(-3) sum_i E[|X_i|^3].

added_lemmas.statement: For the notation in def:variance-envelope-handle, define T_n(y)=n^(-1) sum_i sum_{r in {+,-}} s_r E_{ir} y_{ir}/pi_{ir}. For every fixed y in R^(2n), Var_pi(T_n(y))=Q_n(y). For the true target vector y^0_{i+}=Y_in(h_n^+) and y^0_{i-}=Y_in(h_n^-), T_n(y^0)=hat_tau_HT_n and Q_n(y^0)=sigma_n^2.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: For fixed y, expand the variance of T_n(y). The covariance between E_{ir}y_{ir}/pi_{ir} and E_{js}y_{js}/pi_{js} is y_{ir}y_{js}[E_pi(E_{ir}E_{js})/(pi_{ir}pi_{js})-1]. By the definition of pi_{ij}^{rs}, including the diagonal convention for i=j in def:variance-envelope-handle, E_pi(E_{ir}E_{js})=pi_{ij}^{rs}. Multiplying by n^(-2)s_r s_s and summing over i,j,r,s gives exactly Q_n(y). For the true vector y^0, ass:exposure-consistency gives E_{ir}Y_in^obs=E_{ir}y^0_{ir}, so T_n(y^0) is the Horvitz-Thompson contrast in def:ht-contrast. Therefore Q_n(y^0)=Var_pi(T_n(y^0))=Var_pi(hat_tau_HT_n)=sigma_n^2.
